notes: Friulian: heavy contact with German (Habsburg domination) and Slovene (eastern border zone). Third slot is the weakest — French is a marginal influence via Romance koiné and Napoleonic period.
Ligurian (Genoese): mediterranean trade contacts — Provençal-French via Liguria, Spanish ports, and Catalan trade routes (Mediterranean commercial empire of the Crown of Aragon).
Lombard: Habsburg rule of Lombardy + French (Cisalpine Republic, Napoleonic period) + Spanish rule of Milan (1535-1714).
Sardinian: long Aragonese/Spanish rule (1297-1714) — strong Catalan + Spanish substrate. Third slot French is weaker (post-Savoy/Piedmontese influence and brief Napoleonic period).
Sicilian: Norman rule (French) + Aragonese rule (Spanish/Catalan). Historical Greek and Arabic substrate are NOT in our candidate set so cannot be tested.
Venetan: Habsburg rule of Veneto (1815-1866) + Slovene contact along eastern border + French influence via Napoleonic Italian Republic (1797-1814).
